Auto-generated developer documentation

AutoDocX

Developer-focused documentation generated from project scan data and code structure. Use this page to understand the project, inspect APIs, review modules, and see class/function signatures.

Provider: openai Model: gpt-5-mini Scan: 20260707_115957_8888dccb Understanding: 20260707_120123_3caeb2cd Created: 2026-07-07T12:01:23.510131+00:00

Overview

This project provides an automated developer-documentation pipeline named AutoDocX. It scans a local codebase, extracts structural developer-facing metadata (modules, symbols, FastAPI routes, imports), analyzes relationships, optionally augments with runtime/tooling context (user notes and screenshots), uses a configured LLM to generate narrative understanding, and produces standalone HTML documentation files. The application exposes a FastAPI-based HTTP API and a Streamlit UI client for running scans, creating/updating documentation, comparing scans, retrieving saved scans/understandings/documents, and attaching runtime context. Storage is file-based under the workspace directory (scans, understandings, documents).
API paths, handlers, signatures, parameters, classes, and methods are taken from parser data where available, not guessed by the LLM.

Quickstart

Start the FastAPI server, then open the generated API docs or call the project documentation sync endpoint.

Run the application PowerShell / terminal 
python -m uvicorn app.main:app --reload
Generate documentation Python request example 
import requests

response = requests.post(
    "http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/projects/documentation/sync",
    json={
        "project_path": r"C:\path\to\AutoDocX"
    },
    timeout=900,
)

response.raise_for_status()
print(response.json())

Architecture

Layers and how data flows: • Presentation: ui.streamlit_app (Streamlit helper functions) acts as a client and calls the FastAPI endpoints. app.main exposes basic health routes. • API / Orchestration: app.api.project_routes implements endpoints (scan, sync, sync-with-context, compare, understand, document generation and retrieval). Routes orchestrate service calls, handle temporary uploads, and return typed Pydantic responses. • Services: scanning/parsing/analyzing (ProjectScanner -> PythonCodeParser -> ProjectAnalyzer) produce a structured scan. Context builders (ProjectContextBuilder) compact that for LLMs. ProjectUnderstandingService and RuntimeContextAnalyzer call LLM providers (via LLMProviderFactory) to produce narrative understandings and runtime analyses. DocumentBuilder converts saved understandings into HTML. • Persistence: ScanStorage, UnderstandingStorage, DocumentStorage persist JSON metadata and files under workspace/* directories. DocumentStorage also manages runtime assets (screenshots) and document HTML files. • LLM Providers: provider implementations (OpenAIProvider, AnthropicProvider) implement a BaseLLMProvider interface; the factory normalizes provider selection and constructs provider instances. Typical flow: API request -> ProjectScanner parses and ProjectAnalyzer builds project analysis -> ScanStorage saves scan -> optional comparator compares scans -> ProjectUnderstandingService (calls LLM) generates understanding -> UnderstandingStorage saves it -> DocumentBuilder builds HTML -> DocumentStorage saves document and assets.

Runtime and Tooling Context

Runtime summary

This repository is a Python-based documentation automation service (named AutoDocX in the code) with two runtime components observed: a FastAPI backend (app.main / app.api.project_routes) served with Uvicorn on port 8000, and a Streamlit UI (ui/streamlit_app.py) served on port 8501. The backend scans local Python projects, stores scan results and LLM-generated ‘understandings’, compares scans, and builds/saves static HTML developer documentation. The system supports uploading ordered runtime/context blocks and screenshots; an optional vision-capable LLM analyzes screenshots together with code context. Storage appears to be local filesystem-based under a workspace directory (scans, understandings, documents).

Tooling stack

Python

Primary implementation language and runtime for backend and UI

Evidence: Project contains many .py files (app/*, ui/streamlit_app.py) and Python-specific constructs (BaseModel, FastAPI router, Uvicorn start).

FastAPI

Backend HTTP API that exposes endpoints for scanning, comparing, generating documentation, uploading runtime context, and LLM interactions

Evidence: app.api.project_routes defines many router endpoints (e.g. POST /api/projects/documentation/sync, POST /api/projects/documentation/sync-with-context), handlers use response_model and status codes consistent with FastAPI.

Uvicorn

ASGI server used to run the FastAPI backend in development

Evidence: Screenshot logs show command 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload' and info lines 'Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000'.

Streamlit

Local GUI (developer-facing) used to call backend endpoints and build multipart runtime/context payloads including screenshots

Evidence: ui/streamlit_app.py exists and screenshot shows the command 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' starting on 0.0.0.0:8501; the streamlit module contains functions like sync_documentation_with_context and build_context_blocks_payload.

Local filesystem workspace (scans/understandings/documents)

Persistent storage of project scans, generated understandings, and generated HTML documentation

Evidence: Services document_storage, scan_storage and understanding_storage docstrings reference saving to 'workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json' and workspace/understandings/<project_name>/<understanding_id>.json. app.core.config.create_required_directories creates WORKSPACE_PATH, SCANS_PATH, UNDERSTANDINGS_PATH, DOCUMENTS_PATH.

LLM provider integrations (OpenAI/Anthropic/Claude inferred)

Generate narrative project understanding and (optionally) analyze runtime screenshots using vision-capable models

Evidence: app.core.config has get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model. app.services.llm contains OpenAIProvider and AnthropicProvider classes and a factory. project_understanding_service.generate_understanding and runtime_context_analyzer.analyze are called from routes.

requests (HTTP client)

Used by the Streamlit UI to call the FastAPI backend endpoints

Evidence: ui/streamlit_app.py functions parse_json_response, sync_documentation use requests.post and requests.get.

Runtime flow

1

Start backend API

Developer runs the FastAPI app with Uvicorn (typically using the command 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload'). Uvicorn serves the API at 127.0.0.1:8000 by default in the screenshots. The backend registers multiple project-related endpoints (scan, compare, understand, generate document, sync-with-context).

Evidence: app.main contains FastAPI app routes and app.api.project_routes defines endpoints. Screenshot 2 shows 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload' and 'Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000' plus GET logs for /api/projects/*.

2

Start Streamlit UI (optional developer GUI)

Developer runs the Streamlit application to provide a GUI for calling the backend and uploading runtime/context blocks with screenshots. Streamlit serves on port 8501 and builds multipart requests (files + JSON) for the endpoint that accepts context and screenshots.

Evidence: ui/streamlit_app.py contains functions build_context_blocks_payload and sync_documentation_with_context; screenshot 1 shows 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' started with Local URL: http://localhost:8501.

3

Scan project or sync documentation (API call)

A call to POST /api/projects/documentation/sync or /api/projects/documentation/sync-with-context triggers a project scan (ProjectScanner) which parses Python files and computes project-level stats and relationships, then save the scan to scan storage. If runtime context blocks and screenshots were provided, they are saved/prepared and merged with previous context.

Evidence: project_routes.sync_project_documentation and sync_project_documentation_with_context call project_scanner.scan_project and scan_storage.save_scan. ui/streamlit_app.sync_documentation_with_context builds multipart payloads and posts to the sync-with-context endpoint.

4

Compare scans and decide action

If a previous document exists, the backend retrieves the previous scan and uses ScanComparator.compare_scans to produce a comparison. If changes are detected (or runtime context changed), the backend generates a new understanding and document; otherwise it returns the unchanged document metadata.

Evidence: sync_project_documentation/sync-with-context docstrings and code call document_storage.list_documents, scan_storage.get_scan, scan_comparator.compare_scans and then either _generate_and_save_understanding/_generate_and_save_document or return unchanged response.

5

Generate understanding via LLM provider

When needed the system builds an LLM-ready project context (ProjectUnderstandingService). It selects provider via get_llm_provider, obtains credentials through get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model, then calls provider-specific generate functions. The resulting structured understanding is saved by UnderstandingStorage.

Evidence: Routes call get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model and app.services.project_understanding_service.generate_understanding and understanding_storage.save_understanding. app.services.llm factory contains OpenAIProvider and AnthropicProvider.

6

Build HTML documentation and store assets

DocumentBuilder.build_html creates a complete developer documentation HTML file that includes runtime/context blocks and screenshots when provided. DocumentStorage.save_document stores the HTML and runtime assets; the document file can be fetched via GET /api/projects/{project_name}/documents/{document_id}/html.

Evidence: generate_project_document and update_project_document call document_builder.build_html and document_storage.save_document; get_project_document_html returns a FileResponse from document_storage.get_document_file.

7

Optional runtime context screenshot analysis

If the submitted runtime context contains screenshots or text, _analyze_runtime_context_with_llm can call runtime_context_analyzer.analyze with a vision-capable model to extract structured, non-hallucinated insights from screenshots combined with the project scan.

Evidence: sync_project_documentation_with_context calls _analyze_runtime_context_with_llm which calls runtime_context_analyzer.analyze(stored_scan=..., runtime_context=..., provider_name=..., api_key=..., model=...). The analyzer docstring mentions vision-capable LLM usage.

Screenshot insights

Streamlit UI running at port 8501

What it shows: A developer started the Streamlit UI using 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' and the server bound to 0.0.0.0:8501 (Local URL http://localhost:8501).

Why it matters: Confirms the project includes a Streamlit frontend that communicates with the backend and can submit runtime context blocks and screenshots (ui/streamlit_app.py functions). The Streamlit UI is the expected way to attach screenshots and context for sync-with-context flows.

Uvicorn FastAPI backend on port 8000 handling requests

What it shows: Console output from 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload' showing Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000 and multiple successful GET requests for /api/projects/AutoDocX/documents with status 200.

Why it matters: Confirms the FastAPI backend is running locally and serving the documented endpoints used by the UI (scan, sync, documents). The GET logs show document listing endpoints are reachable and returning data.

Auto-reload behavior when modifying routes

What it shows: Uvicorn/StatReload warnings and repeated 'Shutting down' / 'Started server process' messages triggered by changes in app/api/project_routes.py.

Why it matters: Indicates development mode (--reload) is used. File changes trigger reloads which is helpful for iterative development but means the process will restart on edits; tests and long-running worker simulation should account for reload interruptions.

Operational notes

  • Backend API default observed at http://127.0.0.1:8000 (Uvicorn) — verify if a different host/port is configured via environment or run args.
  • Streamlit UI default observed at http://localhost:8501. Streamlit acts as a client that builds JSON + file multipart payloads and uses requests to call the API endpoints (see ui/streamlit_app.build_context_blocks_payload and sync_documentation_with_context).
  • Ensure required workspace directories exist or run create_required_directories() from app.core.config; code expects workspace/scans, workspace/understandings, workspace/documents.
  • Set environment variables required to call LLM providers. The code expects a provider name via LLM_PROVIDER and provider-specific API keys/models using names resolved in get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model (check your .env). Without valid keys the endpoints that call LLMs will fail (HTTP 502/500).
  • Uploads: use the sync-with-context endpoint to submit context blocks and screenshots. Streamlit UI already builds correct multipart payloads mapping screenshot_index in each block to uploaded files.
  • Generated HTML docs are stored locally and served via GET /api/projects/{project_name}/documents/{document_id}/html (FileResponse).
  • Developer workflow suggested by evidence: run uvicorn backend, run streamlit UI, open Streamlit, select project folder path, optionally paste screenshots/context, and press sync to produce/upgrade documentation.

Runtime risks and gaps

high

.env and LLM credentials missing or misconfigured

The code requires LLM provider selection and API keys (get_llm_provider, get_llm_api_key). If LLM_PROVIDER or provider API keys are missing or wrong the understanding and runtime screenshot analysis endpoints will error. The repository scan does not show a sample .env or exact environment variable names in full, so developers must locate or create the .env and supply keys.

medium

Local filesystem storage scalability and backups

DocumentStorage/ScanStorage/UnderstandingStorage save files to the local workspace directory. This is fine for local development but may not be appropriate for multi-user, long-term storage or CI; no remote object store configuration is visible in the scan.

high

No authentication/authorization visible for API endpoints

The project routes handle file uploads and LLM usage but the scan shows no authentication middleware or API key enforcement on endpoints. If deployed to a networked environment this could expose internal code metadata and LLM costs.

medium

Vision-capable LLM model selection and costs unclear

Runtime screenshot analysis depends on a 'vision-capable' LLM model. The code references model selection but does not document which provider/model is required for vision features and what costs/latency to expect.

low

Unclear behavior around deduplication and asset batching for screenshots

Functions exist to merge/ dedupe screenshots and context blocks (_merge_screenshot_lists, _screenshot_signature). The exact rules and migration of asset directories (asset_batch_id, asset_directory) are not fully visible from the provided scan; this may need verification when uploading many screenshots or updating documents.

low

Not visible from the provided context: Docker/Terraform/CI deployment

User mentions Docker/Temporal in notes and context blocks, but the code scan and screenshots only show local Streamlit and Uvicorn runs. There is no visible Dockerfile, compose or deployment configuration in the provided scan, so containerized deployment is not confirmed.

Status code status.HTTP_200_OK

Request example

Example requests 
import requests

response = requests.post(
    "http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/projects/AutoDocX/understand",
    json={
    "scan_id": "scan_id"
},
    timeout=900,
)

response.raise_for_status()
print(response.json())

from app.api.project_routes import build_project_context

# Use build_project_context based on the module API.

Functions

_parse_context_blocks_json

def _parse_context_blocks_json(context_blocks_json: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:

Parse ordered runtime context blocks sent by the Streamlit UI. Expected JSON format: [ { "title": "Docker runtime", "text": "This shows Docker containers.", "screenshot_index": 0 }, { "title": "Temporal workflow", "text": "This shows Temporal execution.", "screenshot_index": 1 } ]

_build_runtime_context_from_blocks

def _build_runtime_context_from_blocks(additional_context: str, context_blocks_json: str, runtime_assets: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:

Build the final runtime context object used by DocumentBuilder and DocumentStorage. This connects each context block to the correct uploaded/pasted screenshot using screenshot_index.

_merge_runtime_contexts

def _merge_runtime_contexts(previous_runtime_context: dict[str, Any] | None, new_runtime_context: dict[str, Any] | None) -> dict[str, Any]:

Merge old runtime context with the newly submitted runtime context. This allows every new document version to carry forward old screenshots/text and add new ones.

Called function Call details Why it matters

_screenshot_signature

def _screenshot_signature(screenshot: dict[str, Any]) -> str:

sync_project_documentation

def sync_project_documentation(request: DocumentationSyncRequest) -> DocumentationSyncResponse:

Complete documentation workflow. The endpoint: 1. Scans and saves the project. 2. Finds the latest document. 3. Creates initial documentation when no document exists. 4. Compares scans when a document exists. 5. Creates a new version when changes exist. 6. Returns the existing document when there are no changes.

Calls made by this function

Called function Call details Why it matters
router.post router.post('/documentation/sync', response_model=DocumentationSyncResponse, status_code=status.HTTP_200_OK, summary='Create or update project documentation using one request') Calls router.post, which appears to support the post step in this function.
project_scanner.scan_project project_scanner.scan_project(project_path=request.project_path) Scans files, folders, or project structure.
scan_storage.save_scan scan_storage.save_scan(scan_result=scan_result) Writes or persists output produced by the workflow.
str.strip str.strip(...) Calls str.strip, which appears to support the strip step in this function.
scan_storage.get_scan scan_storage.get_scan(project_name=project_name, scan_id=new_scan_id) Scans files, folders, or project structure.
document_storage.list_documents document_storage.list_documents(project_name=project_name) Calls document_storage.list_documents, which appears to support the list documents step in this function.
scan_comparator.compare_scans scan_storage.get_scan(project_name=project_name, scan_id=old_scan_id) Scans files, folders, or project structure.
comparison_result.get scan_comparator.compare_scans(old_stored_scan=old_stored_scan, new_stored_scan=new_stored_scan) Calls comparison_result.get, which appears to support the get step in this function.
bool comparison_result.get('summary', {}) Calls bool, which appears to support the bool step in this function.
_generate_and_save_understanding bool(comparison_summary.get('has_changes', comparison_contains_changes(comparison_result))) Writes or persists output produced by the workflow.

sync_project_documentation_with_context

def sync_project_documentation_with_context(project_path: str = Form(..., min_length=1, description='Absolute path of the project whose documentation should be created or updated'), additional_context: str = Form(default='', description='Optional general project context that is not visible from source code.'), context_blocks_json: str = Form(default='[]', description='JSON list of ordered runtime context blocks. Each block can contain title, text, and screenshot_index.'), screenshots: list[UploadFile] | None = File(default=None, description='Optional screenshots from Docker, Temporal, Swagger, Cortex, ServiceNow, dashboards, logs, or other project tooling.')) -> DocumentationSyncResponse:

Complete documentation workflow with external runtime/tooling context. This endpoint keeps the original code documentation pipeline but also accepts human-written context and screenshots. The extra context is added to the final HTML documentation under Runtime and Tooling Context.

Calls made by this function

SCANS_PATH - WORKSPACE_PATH / 'scans'
UNDERSTANDINGS_PATH - WORKSPACE_PATH / 'understandings'
DOCUMENTS_PATH - WORKSPACE_PATH / 'documents'

app.services.code_parser

app/services/code_parser.py

`app.services.code_parser` contains 1 class(es) 0 sync function(s) 0 async function(s) 0 constant(s) 0 API route(s) and 0 internal dependency link(s).

Public symbols

PythonCodeParser

Usage pattern

Import example Python 
from app.services.code_parser import PythonCodeParser

# Use PythonCodeParser based on the module API.

Classes

PythonCodeParser

class PythonCodeParser:

Reads a Python file and extracts developer-documentation-ready structure from it. Extracted information: • imports • module constants • functions • async functions • classes • class attributes • class methods • function/method signatures • arguments with annotations/defaults • return annotations • docstrings • decorators • FastAPI routes • source previews • content hashes • syntax errors

Attributes

Name Type Default / Value
HTTP_METHODS - {'get', 'post', 'put', 'patch', 'delete', 'options', 'head', 'trace', 'websocket'}

Methods

parse_file

def parse_file(self, project_root: Path, relative_file_path: str) -> dict[str, Any]:

Implements the parse file operation for this module. It coordinates the workflow by calling source_code.splitlines. The call is made as source_code.splitlines(...). It returns dict[str, Any] used by the surrounding workflow.

_extract_import

def _extract_import(node: ast.Import) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:

Implements the extract import operation for this module. It coordinates the workflow by calling imports.append. The call is made as imports.append({'type': 'import', 'module': imported_name.name, 'name': None, 'alias': imported_name.asname, 'line': getattr(node, 'lineno', None), 'statement': f'import {imported_name.name}' if imported_name.asname is None else f'import {imported_name.name} as {imported_name.asname}'}). It returns list[dict[str, Any]] used by the surrounding workflow.

Calls made by this function

Called function Call details Why it matters

def _extract_import_from(node: ast.ImportFrom) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:

Implements the extract import from operation for this module. It coordinates the workflow by calling imports.append. The call is made as imports.append({'type': 'from_import', 'module': module_name, 'name': imported_name.name, 'alias': imported_name.asname, 'line': getattr(node, 'lineno', None), 'statement': f'from {module_name} import {imported_name.name}' if imported_name.asname is None else f'from {module_name} import {imported_name.name} as {imported_name.asname}'}). It returns list[dict[str, Any]] used by the surrounding workflow.

_calculate_node_hash

def _calculate_node_hash(node: ast.AST) -> str:

Creates a stable SHA-256 hash from an AST node. Formatting-only changes generally do not change this hash.

app.services.document_builder

app/services/document_builder.py

`app.services.document_builder` contains 1 class(es) 0 sync function(s) 0 async function(s) 0 constant(s) 0 API route(s) and 0 internal dependency link(s).

Public symbols

DocumentBuilder

Usage pattern

Import example Python 
from app.services.document_builder import DocumentBuilder

# Use DocumentBuilder based on the module API.

Classes

DocumentBuilder

class DocumentBuilder:

Builds a standalone professional developer documentation HTML from a saved project understanding. The output is closer to developer documentation pages: • left navigation • main article content • right table of contents • API reference cards • parameter tables • module reference cards • class/function/method signatures • code examples • clean callouts

Methods

build_html

app.services.document_storage

app/services/document_storage.py

`app.services.document_storage` contains 1 class(es) 0 sync function(s) 0 async function(s) 0 constant(s) 0 API route(s) and 1 internal dependency link(s).

Public symbols

DocumentStorage

Usage pattern

Import example Python 
from app.services.document_storage import DocumentStorage

# Use DocumentStorage based on the module API.

Classes

DocumentStorage

class DocumentStorage:

Stores generated HTML documentation files, document metadata, and optional runtime context assets such as uploaded screenshots.

Attributes

Name Type Default / Value
ALLOWED_SCREENSHOT_EXTENSIONS - {'.png', '.jpg', '.jpeg', '.webp'}

Methods

__init__

save_document

def save_document(self, project_name: str, understanding_id: str, scan_id: str, html_content: str, previous_document_id: str | None = None, update_type: str = 'initial', comparison_summary: dict[str, Any] | None = None, runtime_context: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:

Save one generated HTML document and its metadata. runtime_context is optional and is used for extra project information that is not visible from code alone, such as Docker, Temporal, Cortex, ServiceNow, screenshots, dashboards, or deployment notes.

prepare_runtime_assets

def prepare_runtime_assets(self, project_name: str, files: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:

Save uploaded screenshot files under the project document assets directory. Expected file item format: { "filename": "docker.png", "content_type": "image/png", "file_path": Path("temporary_upload.png") } This method is intentionally framework-neutral. FastAPI routes can first save UploadFile objects to temporary files, then pass those paths here.

_normalize_runtime_context

def _normalize_runtime_context(runtime_context: dict[str, Any] | None) -> dict[str, Any]:

Normalize optional runtime/tooling context before writing it into document metadata. Supports: 1. Raw user context: additional_context context_blocks screenshots 2. LLM-analyzed runtime understanding: runtime_understanding

_sanitize_name

def _sanitize_name(value: str) -> str:

Implements the sanitize name operation for this module. It coordinates the workflow by calling re.sub. The call is made as re.sub('[^a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+', '_', value.strip()). It returns str used by the surrounding workflow.

Anthropic Claude implementation using the Messages API.

generate_json

def generate_json(self, system_prompt: str, user_prompt: str) -> dict[str, Any]:

Implements the generate json operation for this module. It coordinates the workflow by calling self.client.messages.create. The call is made as self.client.messages.create(model=self.model, max_tokens=8000, temperature=0, system=system_prompt, messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': user_prompt}]). It returns dict[str, Any] used by the surrounding workflow.

def parse_json_response(response_text: str) -> dict[str, Any]:

app.services.llm.prompts

`app.services.llm.prompts` contains 0 class(es) 1 sync function(s) 0 async function(s) 1 constant(s) 0 API route(s) and 0 internal dependency link(s).

Public symbols

PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPTbuild_project_understanding_prompt

Usage pattern

Import example Python 
from app.services.llm.prompts import PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT

# Use PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT based on the module API.

Constants

Name Type Value
PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT - '\nYou are AutoDocX, a professional developer documentation engine.\n\nYou receive deterministic project context extracted from source code.\nThe context may include modules, classes, functions, method signatures,\nFastAPI routes, dependencies, docstrings, and project statistics.\n\nYour job is to generate clean developer documentation metadata.\n\nImportant rules:\n\n1. Do not invent files, functions, classes, APIs, databases, services,\n dependencies, parameters, return values, or behavior.\n2. Keep module names, function names, class names, method names, and API\n paths exactly as supplied.\n3. Write like professional developer docs, not like an audit report.\n4. Do not write phrases like "Confirmed", "Reasonable inference",\n "Based on the context", or "It appears".\n5. If something is unclear, write a short neutral sentence such as:\n "The provided scan does not expose this detail."\n6. Do not include Markdown code fences.\n7. Do not include API keys, credentials, secrets, or environment values.\n8. Return exactly one valid JSON object.\n9. Do not return text before or after the JSON object.\n\nThe JSON object must follow this exact structure:\n\n{\n "project_summary": "string",\n "architecture_overview": "string",\n "execution_flow": [\n {\n "step": 1,\n "title": "string",\n "description": "string",\n "related_modules": ["string"]\n }\n ],\n "module_responsibilities": [\n {\n "module": "string",\n "file": "string",\n "responsibility": "string",\n "important_symbols": ["string"]\n }\n ],\n "api_overview": [\n {\n "method": "string",\n "path": "string",\n "handler": "string",\n "purpose": "string"\n }\n ],\n "key_dependencies": [\n {\n "source": "string",\n "target": "string",\n "purpose": "string"\n }\n ],\n "risks_and_gaps": [\n {\n "title": "string",\n "description": "string",\n "severity": "low | medium | high"\n }\n ],\n "recommended_document_sections": [\n {\n "title": "string",\n "purpose": "string"\n }\n ]\n}\n\nStyle requirements:\n\n- project_summary should be 1 to 2 concise paragraphs.\n- architecture_overview should explain the application layers and how\n code moves through them.\n- execution_flow should describe runtime flow in practical developer terms.\n- module_responsibilities should be short and specific.\n- api_overview should explain what each endpoint is used for.\n- risks_and_gaps should focus on real engineering gaps such as\n authentication, error handling, retries, persistence, testing,\n configuration, observability, and deployment readiness.\n- recommended_document_sections should suggest useful developer docs,\n not generic business report sections.\n'.strip()

Functions

app.services.project_analyzer

Public symbols

ProjectAnalyzer

Usage pattern

Import example Python 
from app.services.project_analyzer import ProjectAnalyzer

# Use ProjectAnalyzer based on the module API.

Classes

ProjectAnalyzer

class ProjectAnalyzer:

Builds project-level relationships from parsed Python files. The analyzer: • maps project modules • detects internal module dependencies • collects functions, async functions, classes, methods and constants • preserves signatures, arguments, returns, docstrings and source previews • discovers FastAPI router prefixes • discovers include_router relationships • builds complete FastAPI endpoint paths • creates developer documentation references • creates project-level statistics

Methods

analyze_project

app.services.project_context_builder

app/services/project_context_builder.py

`app.services.project_context_builder` contains 1 class(es) 0 sync function(s) 0 async function(s) 0 constant(s) 0 API route(s) and 0 internal dependency link(s).

Public symbols

ProjectContextBuilder

Usage pattern

Import example Python 
from app.services.project_context_builder import ProjectContextBuilder

# Use ProjectContextBuilder based on the module API.

Classes

ProjectContextBuilder

class ProjectContextBuilder:

Converts a complete AutoDocX scan into structured project context. Supported modes: detailed Keeps complete module, symbol, route, import, dependency, source preview, signature, and content-hash information. llm Produces a smaller context intended for LLM documentation generation while still preserving developer-documentation fields such as signatures, arguments, return types, routes, class methods, and docstrings.

Attributes

Name Type Default / Value
SUPPORTED_MODES - {'detailed', 'llm'}
ENTRY_POINT_NAMES - {'main.py', 'app.py', 'server.py', 'manage.py', 'cli.py', '__main__.py'}
CONFIG_FILE_NAMES - {'requirements.txt', 'pyproject.toml', 'setup.py', 'setup.cfg', 'tox.ini', 'pytest.ini', 'dockerfile', 'docker-compose.yml', 'docker-compose.yaml', '.env.example', '.gitignore', 'package.json', 'tsconfig.json', 'alembic.ini'}
DOCUMENTATION_FILE_NAMES - {'readme.md', 'readme.txt', 'contributing.md', 'changelog.md', 'license', 'license.txt'}
SCRIPT_EXTENSIONS - {'.bat', '.ps1', '.sh', '.cmd'}
STANDARD_LIBRARY_MODULES - {'abc', 'ast', 'asyncio', 'collections', 'datetime', 'enum', 'functools', 'hashlib', 'html', 'inspect', 'io', 'json', 'logging', 'os', 'pathlib', 're', 'shutil', 'subprocess', 'sys', 'time', 'typing', 'uuid'}

app.services.project_scanner

app/services/project_scanner.py

`app.services.project_scanner` contains 1 class(es) 0 sync function(s) 0 async function(s) 0 constant(s) 0 API route(s) and 2 internal dependency link(s).

Public symbols

ProjectScanner

Usage pattern

Import example Python 
from app.services.project_scanner import ProjectScanner

# Use ProjectScanner based on the module API.

Classes

ProjectScanner

class ProjectScanner:

Scans and analyzes a local project directory. The scanner: • ignores unnecessary folders and files • lists project files • counts directories • counts file extensions • calculates project size • calculates SHA-256 file hashes • parses Python source files • analyzes project-level relationships

Attributes

Name Type Default / Value
IGNORED_DIRECTORIES - {'.git', '.idea', '.vscode', '.venv', 'venv', 'env', '__pycache__', '.pytest_cache', '.mypy_cache', '.ruff_cache', 'node_modules', 'dist', 'build', 'coverage', 'htmlcov', '.tox', '.next', 'workspace'}
IGNORED_FILES - {'.DS_Store', 'Thumbs.db'}
IGNORED_EXTENSIONS - {'.pyc', '.pyo', '.pyd', '.log', '.tmp', '.temp', '.swp', '.swo'}

Methods

app.services.project_understanding_service

app/services/project_understanding_service.py

`app.services.project_understanding_service` contains 1 class(es) 0 sync function(s) 0 async function(s) 0 constant(s) 0 API route(s) and 4 internal dependency link(s).

Public symbols

ProjectUnderstandingService

Usage pattern

Import example Python 
from app.services.project_understanding_service import ProjectUnderstandingService

# Use ProjectUnderstandingService based on the module API.

Classes

ProjectUnderstandingService

class ProjectUnderstandingService:

Builds LLM-ready context and asks the selected provider to generate structured project understanding. Important: The LLM is used for narrative understanding only. Developer reference data such as API paths, handlers, signatures, parameters, return types, classes, and methods is merged from parser/analyzer output to avoid hallucination.

Attributes

Name Type Default / Value
REQUIRED_UNDERSTANDING_FIELDS - {'project_summary', 'architecture_overview', 'execution_flow', 'module_responsibilities', 'api_overview', 'key_dependencies', 'risks_and_gaps', 'recommended_document_sections'}

_generate_understanding_with_retry

def _generate_understanding_with_retry(self, provider: Any, user_prompt: str) -> dict[str, Any]:

Generate project understanding from the LLM. The first attempt uses the normal prompt. If the LLM returns malformed JSON or misses required fields, retry once with stricter JSON-only instructions.

Calls made by this function

Called function Call details Why it matters
provider.generate_json provider.generate_json(system_prompt=PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT, user_prompt=user_prompt) Generates output data, files, reports, or documentation.

_merge_developer_reference_data

def _merge_developer_reference_data(self, understanding: dict[str, Any], project_context: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:

Merge reliable parser/analyzer data into LLM output. This makes final documentation closer to professional developer docs. API paths, handlers, signatures, classes, methods, and parameters should come from code parsing, not from LLM guesses.

app.services.runtime_context_analyzer

app/services/runtime_context_analyzer.py

`app.services.runtime_context_analyzer` contains 1 class(es) 0 sync function(s) 0 async function(s) 1 constant(s) 0 API route(s) and 2 internal dependency link(s).

Public symbols

RUNTIME_CONTEXT_SYSTEM_PROMPTRuntimeContextAnalyzer

Usage pattern

Import example Python 
from app.services.runtime_context_analyzer import RUNTIME_CONTEXT_SYSTEM_PROMPT

# Use RUNTIME_CONTEXT_SYSTEM_PROMPT based on the module API.

Constants

Name Type Value
RUNTIME_CONTEXT_SYSTEM_PROMPT - '\nYou are AutoDocX Runtime Context Analyzer.\n\nYour job is to analyze a software project using:\n1. Parsed project/code scan context.\n2. User-written runtime/tooling notes.\n3. Screenshots attached by the user.\n\nYou must create practical developer-facing runtime documentation.\n\nImportant:\n- Do not assume the project is Python.\n- Do not assume the project uses FastAPI, Streamlit, Docker, Temporal, Swagger, or any specific tool.\n- Detect the actual language, framework, runtime, commands, ports, services, dashboards, and workflow from the available evidence.\n- If the code scanner provides limited details for a language such as Java, C, C++, Go, JavaScript, TypeScript, or another stack, use filenames, folder names, config files, build files, commands, screenshots, and user notes as supporting evidence.\n- Separate confirmed facts from reasonable inferences.\n- Do not invent tools, services, APIs, databases, or cloud systems that are not supported by code, screenshots, logs, filenames, or user notes.\n\nYou must explain:\n- What the project appears to be.\n- How a developer runs it.\n- What tools or services are involved.\n- How the UI, backend, APIs, workers, jobs, workflows, scripts, or external systems interact.\n- What each screenshot proves.\n- What operational risks, missing setup steps, or unclear areas remain.\n\nUse screenshots as evidence, not decoration.\nUse user notes as hints, but rewrite them into clear technical documentation.\nUse project scan context to connect runtime behavior to real files, modules, endpoints, scripts, commands, or configuration when possible.\n\nReturn only valid JSON.\nDo not use markdown.\nDo not wrap the response in ```json.\n\nReturn this exact JSON structure:\n\n{\n "runtime_summary": "string",\n "tooling_stack": [\n {\n "tool": "string",\n "purpose": "string",\n "evidence": "string"\n }\n ],\n "runtime_flow": [\n {\n "step": 1,\n "title": "string",\n "description": "string",\n "evidence": "string"\n }\n ],\n "screenshot_insights": [\n {\n "title": "string",\n "what_it_shows": "string",\n "why_it_matters": "string"\n }\n ],\n "operational_notes": [\n "string"\n ],\n "risks_or_gaps": [\n {\n "title": "string",\n "description": "string",\n "severity": "low|medium|high"\n }\n ]\n}\n\nWriting rules:\n- Be specific when evidence is clear.\n- Be cautious when evidence is weak.\n- Say "not visible from the provided context" when something is unknown.\n- Do not overfit the explanation to the screenshot only; connect it with the code scan.\n- Do not mention that you are an AI model.\n- Do not include generic filler.\n- Keep the output useful for a developer joining the project.\n'

Classes

RuntimeContextAnalyzer

class RuntimeContextAnalyzer:

Uses a vision-capable LLM to analyze project code context, user-written runtime notes, and screenshots together. This turns pasted screenshots into real project understanding, instead of simply placing raw screenshots into the generated docs.

Attributes

Name Type Default / Value
REQUIRED_FIELDS - {'runtime_summary', 'tooling_stack', 'runtime_flow', 'screenshot_insights', 'operational_notes', 'risks_or_gaps'}

app.services.scan_comparator

app/services/scan_comparator.py

`app.services.scan_comparator` contains 1 class(es) 0 sync function(s) 0 async function(s) 0 constant(s) 0 API route(s) and 0 internal dependency link(s).

Public symbols

ScanComparator

Usage pattern

Import example Python 
from app.services.scan_comparator import ScanComparator

# Use ScanComparator based on the module API.

Classes

ScanComparator

class ScanComparator:

Compares two stored AutoDocX project scans. The comparator detects: • added files • deleted files • modified files • added, removed and modified functions • added, removed and modified classes • added, removed and modified methods • added and removed routes • added and removed internal dependencies

Attributes

Name Type Default / Value
IGNORED_COMPARISON_FIELDS - {'line', 'end_line'}

Methods

ScanStorage

class ScanStorage:

Stores and retrieves AutoDocX project scan results. Scans are saved under: workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json

Implements the get project files operation for this module. It coordinates the workflow by calling sorted. The call is made as sorted(project_directory.glob('*.json'), key=lambda file_path: file_path.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True). It returns list[Path] used by the surrounding workflow.

def _read_file(file_path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:

_sanitize_name

def _sanitize_name(value: str) -> str:

Implements the sanitize name operation for this module. It coordinates the workflow by calling re.sub. The call is made as re.sub('[^a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+', '_', value.strip()). It returns str used by the surrounding workflow.

build_context_blocks_payload

def build_context_blocks_payload(context_blocks: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> tuple[str, list[tuple[str, tuple[str, bytes, str]]]]:

Build multipart form payload for ordered context blocks. The screenshots are sent as files. Each block stores screenshot_index so the backend can connect the correct image to the correct block.

Execution Flow

1

Project scan and parse

ProjectScanner enumerates files, computes file hashes, and parses Python files using PythonCodeParser to extract imports, constants, functions, classes, docstrings, and FastAPI route decorators.

app.services.project_scannerapp.services.code_parser
2

Project analysis and internal model building

ProjectAnalyzer maps modules, discovers router definitions/inclusions, collects symbols and internal dependencies, builds API references and module references, and compiles project-level statistics.

app.services.project_analyzer
3

Persist scan and compare

ScanStorage stores the structured scan JSON. When updating documentation the API optionally retrieves a prior scan and uses ScanComparator to detect documentation-relevant changes.

app.services.scan_storageapp.services.scan_comparator
4

LLM-based understanding generation

ProjectContextBuilder compacts the scan for LLM consumption. ProjectUnderstandingService uses LLMProviderFactory to instantiate the configured provider, sends prompts (via provider implementations), receives JSON output, validates required fields, and merges reliable parser/analyzer reference data into the LLM output.

app.services.project_understanding_serviceapp.services.project_context_builderapp.services.llm.factoryapp.services.llm.baseapp.services.llm.openai_providerapp.services.llm.anthropic_provider
5

Build and save documentation

DocumentBuilder renders a professional HTML document from the validated understanding (optionally including runtime_context). DocumentStorage saves HTML, JSON metadata, and runtime assets (screenshots). UnderstandingStorage records the LLM output.

app.services.document_builderapp.services.document_storageapp.services.understanding_storage
6

Optional runtime context analysis

When the sync-with-context endpoint receives user notes and screenshots, the API prepares assets (DocumentStorage.prepare_runtime_assets), builds runtime context structures, optionally calls RuntimeContextAnalyzer (vision-capable LLM) to produce runtime_understanding, and merges that into the generated document.

app.api.project_routesapp.services.document_storageapp.services.runtime_context_analyzer

Troubleshooting and Production Notes

high

Authentication and secrets management

LLM providers require API keys retrieved from environment via get_llm_api_key. The API and UI have no documented authentication/authorization controls; keys and access control for endpoints are not enforced in the scanned code paths.

medium

Operational observability and monitoring

The code includes print-based retry logging for LLM failures but lacks structured logging, metrics, traces, and alerting integration which are necessary for production troubleshooting and SLOs.

medium

Error handling and retries

LLM calls implement a single retry path for malformed JSON, but network/backoff/retry logic and differentiated error handling for provider rate limits or transient errors are minimal. API responses map storage lookup ValueError to 404 but broader retry and circuit-breaker patterns are absent.

medium

Persistence and concurrent updates

Storage is file-system based under workspace/ paths; there is limited concurrency control or transactional guarantees described for simultaneous writes, which may risk partial writes or races in multi-user environments.

medium

Testing and validation coverage

The scan shows many validation checks but the repository lacks exposed test harnesses or unit/integration test references in the provided context. LLM parsing correctness depends on validated prompts but there is no test suite enforcing expected JSON shapes.

medium

Deployment and configuration

Deployment considerations (process manager, containerization, resource requirements, and secure environment configuration) are not documented by the scanned files. The system depends on external LLM services and adequate network egress, which require operational configuration.

low

Runtime context privacy and storage size

Uploaded screenshots and runtime assets are stored under project documents without explicit retention or privacy controls; large image uploads may increase storage and require quotas or cleanup policies.
